title: Novel Storyboard
creator: Agent Buildprint
summary: Execution-packet remap of the Toonflow-inspired creative pipeline: project workspace, ordered novel ingestion, event extraction, scripts/assets, storyboard flow, media tasks, preview export, and safety gates.
plainDescription: A router-first Buildprint for a novel-to-storyboard workbench. Agents start at BUILDPRINT.md and blueprint.yaml, load one active capability at a time, write proof to an evidence ledger, and keep claims unqualified until proof exists.
promise: A Mapper execution-packet for rebuilding the source-independent novel-to-storyboard workflow without copying Toonflow source or claiming clone, Electron, live-provider, exact UI/canvas, or final stitched-video parity.
whatYouGet: Context map that names the active capability
Six phase files with consolidated contracts and proof gates
Source-surface map tied to product obligations
Provider, state, runtime, safety, acceptance, and evidence ledgers
whatYouNeed: Nothing for deterministic no-network proof mode
Optional AI/image/video provider keys only for live-provider upgrade proof
Security review before public deployment or destructive/admin claims
includes: 02-project-setup.md active context map and read order
03-phases slice index and six phase files
06-contracts/*.md role contracts for delegated architecture, UX/UI, integration/runtime, security, data persistence, and verification
risks: Treating generated/agent-prompt.md as source of truth
Counting deterministic providers as live provider proof
Generic dashboard UI instead of storyboard-first workbench
Using Toonflow source as implementation input
checks: BUILDPRINT.md routes to blueprint.yaml, team/UX gates, and active capability
02-project-setup.md/context-map.yaml blocks reading unrelated phase files upfront
02-project-setup.md/team-stack.yaml, ux-contract.md, and design-quality-bar.md gate UI-bearing phase files
Each phase file has slice entry, active capability contract, active capability implementation path, source-evidence-index.yaml, and Proof gate in the active capability
Claim status remains PROOF_REQUIRED until browser, provider, persistence, security, and clean-room proof rows pass

trustBadges.label: Capability packet
trustBadges.detail: Router, context map, phase files, acceptance gates, and evidence ledger are separated.

trustBadges.label: One-slice-at-a-time execution
trustBadges.detail: Agents load the active capability first and consult the slice index only after proof.

trustBadges.label: Clean-room boundary
trustBadges.detail: Derived from Toonflow evidence, but implementation must not use Toonflow source as input.

trustBadges.label: Proof required
trustBadges.detail: Runtime/provider/persistence/security/clean-room proof still gates any qualification claim.

copyPrompt: Use the Novel Storyboard Buildprint. First bootstrap exact snapshots: agb start https://agent-buildprint.com/buildprints/novel-storyboard/package.json . If agb is not installed, clone https://github.com/DomEscobar/agent-buildprint and run node agent-buildprint/bin/agb.js start https://agent-buildprint.com/buildprints/novel-storyboard/package.json . Then read .buildprint/next-agent.md and continue. Do not write Buildprint snapshots manually. The bootstrapped packet is the source of truth — follow its read order (BUILDPRINT.md, 01-questions.md, 02-project-setup.md, blueprint.yaml, 03-phases/phase-index.yaml, 04-evaluation.md, and the active phase files) instead of restating its rules here.
